problem: Latest options-MC generation is repeatedly emitting byte-identical decision artifacts for unchanged inputs, creating false confidence (apparent sample size inflation), alert/log noise, and unnecessary compute/storage burn. This is a reliability and profitability risk because operators can misread repeated identical outputs as new evidence instead of stale state.

evidence.symptom: EV/POP/CVaR and gate outcomes are unchanged across repeated artifacts despite new file timestamps.

required_change: Add an input-change/idempotency guard before writing options-MC artifacts: if canonical decision inputs are unchanged (underlying snapshot fingerprint, chain snapshot timestamp/hash, strategy config hash, friction config), do not emit a new full artifact.
Emit machine-readable status `NO_NEW_INPUTS` with references to prior artifact id/path when run is skipped.
Introduce a minimum recalc cadence override (e.g., force refresh every N minutes) so operators can still request periodic recomputation intentionally.
Prevent repeated skipped runs from entering ranking/trade-ready pipelines; they should be treated as state-hold, not new opportunity evidence.
acceptance_tests: Unit test: identical canonical inputs across consecutive invocations -> second invocation emits `NO_NEW_INPUTS`, no new full artifact file is created, and prior artifact reference is populated.
Unit test: change in any canonical input (spot snapshot hash, chain timestamp, config hash, or friction config) -> full artifact is generated (not skipped).
Unit test: force-refresh cadence reached -> full artifact is generated even with otherwise unchanged inputs, and telemetry increments forced-refresh counter.
Integration test: replay a fixture with 10 repeated unchanged triggers -> exactly 1 full artifact + 9 `NO_NEW_INPUTS` statuses.
Regression test: ranking/notification path ignores `NO_NEW_INPUTS` runs as new candidates while preserving last valid artifact for display.
constraints: Do not remove existing artifact fields; additive status/telemetry fields only.
Keep deterministic behavior for backtests/replays (same inputs => same skip/full decision).
